description.para: See the XEmacPs_SetOptions(), XEmacPs_ClearOptions() and XEmacPs_GetOptions() for information on how to use options.
The default state of the options are noted and are what the device and driver will be set to after calling XEmacPs_Reset() or XEmacPs_Initialize().

detaileddescription.para: This option defaults to disabled (cleared)

briefdescription.para: Frame larger than 1516 support for Tx & Rx.

detaileddescription.para: This option defaults to disabled (cleared)
This option defaults to disabled (cleared)

briefdescription.para: Enable recognition of flow control frames on Rx This option defaults to enabled (set)
Strip FCS and PAD from incoming frames.

detaileddescription.para: Note: PAD from VLAN frames is not stripped. This option defaults to enabled (set)

briefdescription.para: Generate FCS field and add PAD automatically for outgoing frames.

detaileddescription.para: This option defaults to disabled (cleared)

briefdescription.para: Enable Length/Type error checking for incoming frames.

detaileddescription.para: When this option is set, the MAC will filter frames that have a mismatched type/length field and if XEMACPS_REPORT_RXERR_OPTION is set, the user is notified when these types of frames are encountered. When this option is cleared, the MAC will allow these types of frames to be received.
This option defaults to disabled (cleared)
This option defaults to enabled (set)

briefdescription.para: Enable the receiver This option defaults to enabled (set)
Allow reception of the broadcast address This option defaults to enabled (set)
Allows reception of multicast addresses programmed into hash This option defaults to disabled (clear)
Enable the RX checksum offload This option defaults to enabled (set)
Enable the TX checksum offload This option defaults to enabled (set)
Default options set when device is initialized or reset.

description.para: These constants are used as parameters to XEmacPs_SetHandler()
These callbacks are invoked in interrupt context.

briefdescription.para: Callback invoked when frame(s) have been sent or received in interrupt driven DMA mode.

detaileddescription.para: To set the send callback, invoke XEmacPs_SetHandler().

parameterdescription.para: is user data assigned when the callback was set.

simplesect.para: See xemacps_hw.h for bitmasks definitions and the device hardware spec for further information on their meaning.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* XEmacPs_ErrHandler)(void *CallBackRef, u8 Direction, u32 ErrorWord)
memberdef.name: XEmacPs_ErrHandler

briefdescription.para: Callback when an asynchronous error occurs.

detaileddescription.para: To set this callback, invoke XEmacPs_SetHandler() with XEMACPS_HANDLER_ERROR in the HandlerType parameter.

parameterdescription.para: is user data assigned when the callback was set.
defines either receive or transmit error(s) has occurred.
definition varies with Direction

description.para: These are used by several functions and callbacks that need to specify whether an operation specifies a send or receive channel.

briefdescription.para: Flush a packet from Rx SRAM.
Do not copy pause Frames to memory.
Address bits[47:32] bit[31:0] are in BOTTOM.
Mask bit for RX buffer size.
Shift bit for RX buffer size.

sectiondef.header: Interrupt Q1 status register bit definitions

description.para: Bits definitions are same in XEMACPS_ISR_OFFSET, XEMACPS_IER_OFFSET, XEMACPS_IDR_OFFSET, and XEMACPS_IMR_OFFSET

briefdescription.para: Transmit err occurred or no buffers.

memberdef.name: MAX_QUEUES_FEASIBLE
memberdef.initializer: 4 /* Max queues possible on latest device */

detaileddescription.para: The user is required to allocate a structure of this type for every XEmacPs device in the system. A pointer to a structure of this type is then passed to the driver API functions.

briefdescription.para: The XEmacPs_Bd is the type for buffer descriptors (BDs).
This is a stub for the asynchronous callbacks.

detaileddescription.para: The stub is here in case the upper layer forgot to set the handler(s). On initialization, all handlers are set to this callback. It is considered an error for this handler to be invoked.

The initialization entails:
Initialize fields of the XEmacPs instance structure
Reset hardware and apply default options
Configure the DMA channels
The PHY is setup independently from the device. Use the MII or whatever other interface may be present for setup.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
is the device configuration structure containing required hardware build data.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is not utilized, this parameter can be passed in using CfgPtr->Config.BaseAddress to specify the physical base address.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if initialization was successful

briefdescription.para: Start the Ethernet controller as follows:

listitem.para: Enable transmitter if XTE_TRANSMIT_ENABLE_OPTION is set
Enable receiver if XTE_RECEIVER_ENABLE_OPTION is set
Start the SG DMA send and receive channels and enable the device interrupt

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
N/A
Hardware is configured with scatter-gather DMA, the driver expects to start the scatter-gather channels and expects that the user has previously set up the buffer descriptor lists. This function makes use of internal resources that are shared between the Start, Stop, and Set/ClearOptions functions. So if one task might be setting device options while another is trying to start the device, the user is required to provide protection of this shared data (typically using a semaphore).
This function must not be preempted by an interrupt that may service the device.

briefdescription.para: Gracefully stop the Ethernet MAC as follows:

listitem.para: Disable all interrupts from this device
Stop DMA channels
Disable the transmitter and receiver

detaileddescription.para: Device options currently in effect are not changed.
This function will disable all interrupts. Default interrupts settings that had been enabled will be restored when XEmacPs_Start() is called.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
This function makes use of internal resources that are shared between the Start, Stop, SetOptions, and ClearOptions functions. So if one task might be setting device options while another is trying to start the device, the user is required to provide protection of this shared data (typically using a semaphore). Stopping the DMA channels causes this function to block until the DMA operation is complete.

briefdescription.para: Perform a graceful reset of the Ethernet MAC.

detaileddescription.para: Resets the DMA channels, the transmitter, and the receiver.

Steps to reset
Stops transmit and receive channels
Stops DMA
Configure transmit and receive buffer size to default
Clear transmit and receive status register and counters
Clear all interrupt sources
Clear phy (if there is any previously detected) address
Clear MAC addresses (1-4) as well as Type IDs and hash value

All options are placed in their default state. Any frames in the descriptor lists will remain in the lists. The side effect of doing this is that after a reset and following a restart of the device, frames were in the list before the reset may be transmitted or received.

The upper layer software is responsible for re-configuring (if necessary) and restarting the MAC after the reset. Note also that driver statistics are not cleared on reset. It is up to the upper layer software to clear the statistics if needed.

When a reset is required, the driver notifies the upper layer software of this need through the ErrorHandler callback and specific status codes. The upper layer software is responsible for calling this Reset function and then re-configuring the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.

memberdef.argsstring: (XEmacPs *InstancePtr, UINTPTR QPtr, u8 QueueNum, u16 Direction)
memberdef.name: XEmacPs_SetQueuePtr

briefdescription.para: This function sets the start address of the transmit/receive buffer queue.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
is the address of the Queue to be written
is the Buffer Queue Index
indicates Transmit/Receive

simplesect.para: The buffer queue addresses has to be set before starting the transfer, so this function has to be called in prior to XEmacPs_Start()

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: A table contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration table entry corresponding to the given device ID/BaseAddress, or NULL if no match is found.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Install an asynchronous handler function for the given HandlerType:

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
HandlerType
indicates what interrupt handler type is. XEMACPS_HANDLER_DMASEND, XEMACPS_HANDLER_DMARECV and XEMACPS_HANDLER_ERROR.
FuncPointer
is the pointer to the callback function
CallBackRef
is the upper layer callback reference passed back when when the callback function is invoked.
None.

simplesect.para: There is no assert on the CallBackRef since the driver doesn't know what it is.

briefdescription.para: Master interrupt handler for EMAC driver.

detaileddescription.para: This routine will query the status of the device, bump statistics, and invoke user callbacks.
This routine must be connected to an interrupt controller using OS/BSP specific methods.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XEMACPS instance that has caused the interrupt.

detaileddescription.para: The driver should be stopped with XEmacPs_Stop() before changing options.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
are the options to set. Multiple options can be set by OR'ing XTE_*_OPTIONS constants together. Options not specified are not affected.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the options were set successfully
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device has not yet been stopped

simplesect.para: See xemacps.h for a description of the available options.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
are the options to clear. Multiple options can be cleared by OR'ing XEMACPS_*_OPTIONS constants together. Options not specified are not affected.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the options were set successfully
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device has not yet been stopped

simplesect.para: See xemacps.h for a description of the available options.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: A bitmask of XTE_*_OPTION constants. Any bit set to 1 is to be interpreted as a set option.
See xemacps.h for a description of the available options.

briefdescription.para: Set the MAC address for this driver/device.

detaileddescription.para: The address is a 48-bit value. The device must be stopped before calling this function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
is a pointer to a 6-byte MAC address.
is a index to which MAC (1-4) address.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the MAC address was set successfully
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device has not yet been stopped

briefdescription.para: Delete 48-bit MAC addresses in hash table.

detaileddescription.para: The device must be stopped before calling this function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
is a pointer to a 6-byte MAC address.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the HASH MAC address was deleted successfully
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device has not yet been stopped
XST_INVALID_PARAM if the HASH MAC address passed in does not meet requirement after calculation

simplesect.para: Having Aptr be unsigned type prevents the following operations from sign extending.

briefdescription.para: Get the MAC address for this driver/device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
is an output parameter, and is a pointer to a buffer into which the current MAC address will be copied.
is a index to which MAC (1-4) address.

briefdescription.para: Set 48-bit MAC addresses in hash table.

detaileddescription.para: The device must be stopped before calling this function.

The hash address register is 64 bits long and takes up two locations in the memory map. The least significant bits are stored in hash register bottom and the most significant bits in hash register top.

The unicast hash enable and the multicast hash enable bits in the network configuration register enable the reception of hash matched frames. The destination address is reduced to a 6 bit index into the 64 bit hash register using the following hash function. The hash function is an XOR of every sixth bit of the destination address.
da[0] represents the least significant bit of the first byte received, that is, the multicast/unicast indicator, and da[47] represents the most significant bit of the last byte received.
If the hash index points to a bit that is set in the hash register then the frame will be matched according to whether the frame is multicast or unicast.
A multicast match will be signaled if the multicast hash enable bit is set, da[0] is logic 1 and the hash index points to a bit set in the hash register.
A unicast match will be signaled if the unicast hash enable bit is set, da[0] is logic 0 and the hash index points to a bit set in the hash register.
To receive all multicast frames, the hash register should be set with all ones and the multicast hash enable bit should be set in the network configuration register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
is a pointer to a 6-byte MAC address.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the HASH MAC address was set successfully
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device has not yet been stopped
XST_INVALID_PARAM if the HASH MAC address passed in does not meet requirement after calculation

simplesect.para: Having Aptr be unsigned type prevents the following operations from sign extending.

briefdescription.para: Clear the Hash registers for the mac address pointed by AddressPtr.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Get the Hash address for this driver/device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
is an output parameter, and is a pointer to a buffer into which the current HASH MAC address will be copied.

detaileddescription.para: Calculating the divisor:

para.preformatted: f[HOSTCLK] f[MDC] = ----------------- (1 + Divisor) * 2

detaileddescription.para: where f[HOSTCLK] is the bus clock frequency in MHz, and f[MDC] is the MDIO clock frequency in MHz to the PHY. Typically, f[MDC] should not exceed 2.5 MHz. Some PHYs can tolerate faster speeds which means faster access. Here is the table to show values to generate MDC,

para.preformatted: 000 : divide pclk by 8 (pclk up to 20 MHz) 001 : divide pclk by 16 (pclk up to 40 MHz) 010 : divide pclk by 32 (pclk up to 80 MHz) 011 : divide pclk by 48 (pclk up to 120 MHz) 100 : divide pclk by 64 (pclk up to 160 MHz) 101 : divide pclk by 96 (pclk up to 240 MHz) 110 : divide pclk by 128 (pclk up to 320 MHz) 111 : divide pclk by 224 (pclk up to 540 MHz)

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
is the divisor to set. Range is 0b000 to 0b111.

briefdescription.para: XEmacPs_SetOperatingSpeed sets the current operating link speed.

detaileddescription.para: For any traffic to be passed, this speed must match the current MII/GMII/SGMII/RGMII link speed.

parameterdescription.para: references the TEMAC channel on which to operate.
is the speed to set in units of Mbps. Valid values are 10, 100, or 1000. XEmacPs_SetOperatingSpeed ignores invalid values.

briefdescription.para: XEmacPs_GetOperatingSpeed gets the current operating link speed.

detaileddescription.para: This may be the value set by XEmacPs_SetOperatingSpeed() or a hardware default.

parameterdescription.para: references the TEMAC channel on which to operate.

simplesect.para: XEmacPs_GetOperatingSpeed returns the link speed in units of megabits per second.

briefdescription.para: Read the current value of the PHY register indicated by the PhyAddress and the RegisterNum parameters.

detaileddescription.para: The MAC provides the driver with the ability to talk to a PHY that adheres to the Media Independent Interface (MII) as defined in the IEEE 802.3 standard.
Prior to PHY access with this function, the user should have setup the MDIO clock with XEmacPs_SetMdioDivisor().

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XEmacPs instance to be worked on.
is the address of the PHY to be read (supports multiple PHYs)
is the register number, 0-31, of the specific PHY register to read
is an output parameter, and points to a 16-bit buffer into which the current value of the register will be copied.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the PHY was read from successfully
XST_EMAC_MII_BUSY if there is another PHY operation in progress

detaileddescription.para: This function is not thread-safe. The user must provide mutually exclusive access to this function if there are to be multiple threads that can call it.

There is the possibility that this function will not return if the hardware is broken (i.e., it never sets the status bit indicating that the read is done). If this is of concern to the user, the user should provide a mechanism suitable to their needs for recovery.
For the duration of this function, all host interface reads and writes are blocked to the current XEmacPs instance.

memberdef.argsstring: (XEmacPs *InstancePtr, u32 PhyAddress, u32 RegisterNum, u16 PhyData)
memberdef.name: XEmacPs_PhyWrite

briefdescription.para: Write data to the specified PHY register.

detaileddescription.para: The Ethernet driver does not require the device to be stopped before writing to the PHY. Although it is probably a good idea to stop the device, it is the responsibility of the application to deem this necessary. The MAC provides the driver with the ability to talk to a PHY that adheres to the Media Independent Interface (MII) as defined in the IEEE 802.3 standard.
Prior to PHY access with this function, the user should have setup the MDIO clock with XEmacPs_SetMdioDivisor().

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XEmacPs instance to be worked on.
is the address of the PHY to be written (supports multiple PHYs)
is the register number, 0-31, of the specific PHY register to write
is the 16-bit value that will be written to the register

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the PHY was written to successfully. Since there is no error status from the MAC on a write, the user should read the PHY to verify the write was successful.
XST_EMAC_MII_BUSY if there is another PHY operation in progress

detaileddescription.para: This function is not thread-safe. The user must provide mutually exclusive access to this function if there are to be multiple threads that can call it.

There is the possibility that this function will not return if the hardware is broken (i.e., it never sets the status bit indicating that the write is done). If this is of concern to the user, the user should provide a mechanism suitable to their needs for recovery.
For the duration of this function, all host interface reads and writes are blocked to the current XEmacPs instance.

briefdescription.para: Set the Type ID match for this driver/device.

detaileddescription.para: The register is a 32-bit value. The device must be stopped before calling this function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
is type ID to be configured.
is a index to which Type ID (1-4).

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the MAC address was set successfully
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device has not yet been stopped

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if pause frame transmission was initiated
XST_DEVICE_IS_STOPPED if the device has not been started.

briefdescription.para: API to update the Burst length in the DMACR register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XEmacPs instance to be worked on.
is the length in bytes for the dma burst.

simplesect.para: None

briefdescription.para: Check if a common MDIO (Management Data Input/Output) interface is present.

detaileddescription.para: This function evaluates whether the GEM instance uses a shared MDIO interface by comparing the base address of the current instance with the MDIO producer base address. The existence of a valid MDIO producer base address is also checked.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XEmacPs instance being checked.

listitem.para: TRUE if a common MDIO is present (when the base address of the instance differs from the MDIO producer base address, and the producer base address is non-zero).
FALSE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Set this bit to mark the last descriptor in the receive buffer descriptor list.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XEmacPs_BdSetRxWrap(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Sets this bit to mark the last descriptor in the transmit buffer descriptor list.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XEmacPs_BdSetTxWrap(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

memberdef.argsstring: (XEmacPs_BdRing *RingPtr, UINTPTR PhysAddr, UINTPTR VirtAddr, u32 Alignment, u32 BdCount)
memberdef.name: XEmacPs_BdRingCreate

briefdescription.para: Using a memory segment allocated by the caller, create and setup the BD list for the given DMA channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the instance to be worked on.
is the physical base address of user memory region.
is the virtual base address of the user memory region. If address translation is not being utilized, then VirtAddr should be equivalent to PhysAddr.
governs the byte alignment of individual BDs. This function will enforce a minimum alignment of 4 bytes with no maximum as long as it is specified as a power of 2.
is the number of BDs to setup in the user memory region. It is assumed the region is large enough to contain the BDs.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if initialization was successful
XST_NO_FEATURE if the provided instance is a non DMA type channel.

XST_INVALID_PARAM under any of the following conditions: 1) PhysAddr and/or VirtAddr are not aligned to the given Alignment parameter. 2) Alignment parameter does not meet minimum requirements or is not a power of 2 value. 3) BdCount is 0.
XST_DMA_SG_LIST_ERROR if the memory segment containing the list spans over address 0x00000000 in virtual address space.

simplesect.para: Make sure to pass in the right alignment value.

briefdescription.para: Clone the given BD into every BD in the list.

detaileddescription.para: every field of the source BD is replicated in every BD of the list.

This function can be called only when all BDs are in the free group such as they are immediately after initialization with XEmacPs_BdRingCreate(). This prevents modification of BDs while they are in use by hardware or the user.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer of BD ring instance to be worked on.
is the source BD template to be cloned into the list. This BD will be modified.
is either XEMACPS_SEND or XEMACPS_RECV that indicates which direction.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the list was modified.
XST_DMA_SG_NO_LIST if a list has not been created.
XST_DMA_SG_LIST_ERROR if some of the BDs in this channel are under hardware or user control.
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the DMA channel has not been stopped.

briefdescription.para: Reserve locations in the BD list.

detaileddescription.para: The set of returned BDs may be modified in preparation for future DMA transaction(s). Once the BDs are ready to be submitted to hardware, the user must call XEmacPs_BdRingToHw() in the same order which they were allocated here. Example:

para.preformatted: NumBd = 2, Status = XEmacPs_BdRingAlloc(MyRingPtr, NumBd, &MyBdSet),
* Give list to hardware * Status = XEmacPs_BdRingToHw(MyRingPtr, NumBd, MyBdSet),

detaileddescription.para: A more advanced use of this function may allocate multiple sets of BDs. They must be allocated and given to hardware in the correct sequence: * Legal * XEmacPs_BdRingAlloc(MyRingPtr, NumBd1, &MySet1), XEmacPs_BdRingToHw(MyRingPtr, NumBd1, MySet1),

para.preformatted: * Legal * XEmacPs_BdRingAlloc(MyRingPtr, NumBd1, &MySet1), XEmacPs_BdRingAlloc(MyRingPtr, NumBd2, &MySet2), XEmacPs_BdRingToHw(MyRingPtr, NumBd1, MySet1), XEmacPs_BdRingToHw(MyRingPtr, NumBd2, MySet2),

* Not legal * XEmacPs_BdRingAlloc(MyRingPtr, NumBd1, &MySet1), XEmacPs_BdRingAlloc(MyRingPtr, NumBd2, &MySet2), XEmacPs_BdRingToHw(MyRingPtr, NumBd2, MySet2), XEmacPs_BdRingToHw(MyRingPtr, NumBd1, MySet1),

detaileddescription.para: Use the API defined in xemacps_bd.h to modify individual BDs. Traversal of the BD set can be done using XEmacPs_BdRingNext() and XEmacPs_BdRingPrev().

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the BD ring instance to be worked on.
is the number of BDs to allocate
is an output parameter, it points to the first BD available for modification.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the requested number of BDs was returned in the BdSetPtr parameter.
XST_FAILURE if there were not enough free BDs to satisfy the request.

simplesect.para: This function should not be preempted by another XEmacPs_Bd function call that modifies the BD space. It is the caller's responsibility to provide a mutual exclusion mechanism.
Do not modify more BDs than the number requested with the NumBd parameter. Doing so will lead to data corruption and system instability.

briefdescription.para: Fully or partially undo an XEmacPs_BdRingAlloc() operation.

detaileddescription.para: Use this function if all the BDs allocated by XEmacPs_BdRingAlloc() could not be transferred to hardware with XEmacPs_BdRingToHw().

This function helps out in situations when an unrelated error occurs after BDs have been allocated but before they have been given to hardware. An example of this type of error would be an OS running out of resources.

This function is not the same as XEmacPs_BdRingFree(). The Free function returns BDs to the free list after they have been processed by hardware, while UnAlloc returns them before being processed by hardware.
There are two scenarios where this function can be used. Full UnAlloc or Partial UnAlloc. A Full UnAlloc means all the BDs Alloc'd will be returned:
A partial UnAlloc means some of the BDs Alloc'd will be returned:

para.preformatted: Status = XEmacPs_BdRingAlloc(MyRingPtr, 10, &BdPtr), BdsLeft = 10, CurBdPtr = BdPtr,

detaileddescription.para: A partial UnAlloc must include the last BD in the list that was Alloc'd.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
is the number of BDs to allocate
is an output parameter, it points to the first BD available for modification.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the BDs were unallocated.
XST_FAILURE if NumBd parameter was greater that the number of BDs in the preprocessing state.

simplesect.para: This function should not be preempted by another XEmacPs_Bd function call that modifies the BD space. It is the caller's responsibility to provide a mutual exclusion mechanism.

briefdescription.para: Enqueue a set of BDs to hardware that were previously allocated by XEmacPs_BdRingAlloc().

detaileddescription.para: Once this function returns, the argument BD set goes under hardware control. Any changes made to these BDs after this point will corrupt the BD list leading to data corruption and system instability.
The set will be rejected if the last BD of the set does not mark the end of a packet (see XEmacPs_BdSetLast()).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
is the number of BDs in the set.
is the first BD of the set to commit to hardware.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the set of BDs was accepted and enqueued to hardware.
XST_FAILURE if the set of BDs was rejected because the last BD of the set did not have its "last" bit set.
XST_DMA_SG_LIST_ERROR if this function was called out of sequence with XEmacPs_BdRingAlloc().

simplesect.para: This function should not be preempted by another XEmacPs_Bd function call that modifies the BD space. It is the caller's responsibility to provide a mutual exclusion mechanism.

briefdescription.para: Returns a set of BD(s) that have been processed by hardware.

detaileddescription.para: The returned BDs may be examined to determine the outcome of the DMA transaction(s). Once the BDs have been examined, the user must call XEmacPs_BdRingFree() in the same order which they were retrieved here. Example:

para.preformatted: XEmacPs_BdRingFree(MyRingPtr, NumBd, MyBdSet), *Return list* }

detaileddescription.para: A more advanced use of this function may allocate multiple sets of BDs. They must be retrieved from hardware and freed in the correct sequence: * Legal * XEmacPs_BdRingFromHwTx(MyRingPtr, NumBd1, &MySet1), XEmacPs_BdRingFree(MyRingPtr, NumBd1, MySet1),

para.preformatted: * Legal * XEmacPs_BdRingFromHwTx(MyRingPtr, NumBd1, &MySet1), XEmacPs_BdRingFromHwTx(MyRingPtr, NumBd2, &MySet2), XEmacPs_BdRingFree(MyRingPtr, NumBd1, MySet1), XEmacPs_BdRingFree(MyRingPtr, NumBd2, MySet2),

* Not legal * XEmacPs_BdRingFromHwTx(MyRingPtr, NumBd1, &MySet1), XEmacPs_BdRingFromHwTx(MyRingPtr, NumBd2, &MySet2), XEmacPs_BdRingFree(MyRingPtr, NumBd2, MySet2), XEmacPs_BdRingFree(MyRingPtr, NumBd1, MySet1),

detaileddescription.para: If hardware has only partially completed a packet spanning multiple BDs, then none of the BDs for that packet will be included in the results.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
is the maximum number of BDs to return in the set.
is an output parameter, it points to the first BD available for examination.

simplesect.para: The number of BDs processed by hardware. A value of 0 indicates that no data is available. No more than BdLimit BDs will be returned.
Treat BDs returned by this function as read-only.
This function should not be preempted by another XEmacPs_Bd function call that modifies the BD space. It is the caller's responsibility to provide a mutual exclusion mechanism.

briefdescription.para: Returns a set of BD(s) that have been processed by hardware.

detaileddescription.para: The returned BDs may be examined to determine the outcome of the DMA transaction(s). Once the BDs have been examined, the user must call XEmacPs_BdRingFree() in the same order which they were retrieved here. Example:

para.preformatted: NumBd = XEmacPs_BdRingFromHwRx(MyRingPtr, MaxBd, &MyBdSet),
XEmacPs_BdRingFree(MyRingPtr, NumBd, MyBdSet), * Return list * }

detaileddescription.para: A more advanced use of this function may allocate multiple sets of BDs. They must be retrieved from hardware and freed in the correct sequence: * Legal * XEmacPs_BdRingFromHwRx(MyRingPtr, NumBd1, &MySet1), XEmacPs_BdRingFree(MyRingPtr, NumBd1, MySet1),

para.preformatted: * Legal * XEmacPs_BdRingFromHwRx(MyRingPtr, NumBd1, &MySet1), XEmacPs_BdRingFromHwRx(MyRingPtr, NumBd2, &MySet2), XEmacPs_BdRingFree(MyRingPtr, NumBd1, MySet1), XEmacPs_BdRingFree(MyRingPtr, NumBd2, MySet2),

* Not legal * XEmacPs_BdRingFromHwRx(MyRingPtr, NumBd1, &MySet1), XEmacPs_BdRingFromHwRx(MyRingPtr, NumBd2, &MySet2), XEmacPs_BdRingFree(MyRingPtr, NumBd2, MySet2), XEmacPs_BdRingFree(MyRingPtr, NumBd1, MySet1),

detaileddescription.para: If hardware has only partially completed a packet spanning multiple BDs, then none of the BDs for that packet will be included in the results.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
is the maximum number of BDs to return in the set.
is an output parameter, it points to the first BD available for examination.

simplesect.para: The number of BDs processed by hardware. A value of 0 indicates that no data is available. No more than BdLimit BDs will be returned.
Treat BDs returned by this function as read-only.
This function should not be preempted by another XEmacPs_Bd function call that modifies the BD space. It is the caller's responsibility to provide a mutual exclusion mechanism.

briefdescription.para: Frees a set of BDs that had been previously retrieved with XEmacPs_BdRingFromHw().

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
is the number of BDs to free.
is the head of a list of BDs returned by XEmacPs_BdRingFromHw().

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the set of BDs was freed.
XST_DMA_SG_LIST_ERROR if this function was called out of sequence with XEmacPs_BdRingFromHw().

simplesect.para: This function should not be preempted by another XEmacPs_Bd function call that modifies the BD space. It is the caller's responsibility to provide a mutual exclusion mechanism.

briefdescription.para: Check the internal data structures of the BD ring for the provided channel.

detaileddescription.para: The following checks are made:

listitem.para: Is the BD ring linked correctly in physical address space.
Do the internal pointers point to BDs in the ring.
Do the internal counters add up.

detaileddescription.para: The channel should be stopped prior to calling this function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
is either XEMACPS_SEND or XEMACPS_RECV that indicates which direction.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the set of BDs was freed.
XST_DMA_SG_NO_LIST if the list has not been created.
XST_IS_STARTED if the channel is not stopped.
XST_DMA_SG_LIST_ERROR if a problem is found with the internal data structures. If this value is returned, the channel should be reset to avoid data corruption or system instability.

simplesect.para: This function should not be preempted by another XEmacPs_Bd function call that modifies the BD space. It is the caller's responsibility to provide a mutual exclusion mechanism.

briefdescription.para: Reset BD ring head and tail pointers.

parameterdescription.para: is the instance to be worked on.
is the virtual base address of the user memory region.

simplesect.para: Should be called after XEmacPs_Stop()
C-style signature: void XEmacPs_BdRingPtrReset(XEmacPs_BdRing * RingPtr, void *virtaddrloc)

briefdescription.para: This function perform the reset sequence to the given emacps interface by configuring the appropriate control bits in the emacps specific registers.

detaileddescription.para: the emacps reset sequence involves the following steps Disable all the interuupts Clear the status registers Disable Rx and Tx engines Update the Tx and Rx descriptor queue registers with reset values Update the other relevant control registers with reset value

parameterdescription.para: of the interface

simplesect.para: N/A
This function will not modify the slcr registers that are relevant for emacps controller

briefdescription.para: The register offset of a specific queue register is returned by this function.

parameterdescription.para: Basename of the register
Corresponding Queue

simplesect.para: Register offset

briefdescription.para: Set up PCS as follows for every given speed:

listitem.para: Configure HS_MAC register for given speed
Configure serdes rate and speed in USX_CONTROL register
Assert Rx reset signal for GT reset
Wait for PCS_BLOCK_LOCK

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: N/A

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device

listitem.para: true if block synchronization achieved
false if block is out of sync

briefdescription.para: Detect if the device supports High Speeds ( 5G, 10G )

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device

listitem.para: true if the device support High Speed
false if the device doesn't support High Speed

memberdef.name: XEMACPS_MTU
memberdef.initializer: 1500U /* max MTU size of Ethernet frame */

memberdef.name: XEMACPS_MTU_JUMBO
memberdef.initializer: 10240U /* max MTU size of jumbo frame */

memberdef.name: XEMACPS_HDR_VLAN_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: 18U /* size of Ethernet header with VLAN */

detaileddescription.para: This object can be used in the various Ring API functions.

parameterdescription.para: is the DMA channel to operate on.

simplesect.para: TxBdRing attribute
C-style signature: XEmacPs_BdRing XEmacPs_GetTxRing(XEmacPs *InstancePtr)

detaileddescription.para: This object can be used in the various Ring API functions.

parameterdescription.para: is the DMA channel to operate on.

simplesect.para: RxBdRing attribute
C-style signature: XEmacPs_BdRing XEmacPs_GetRxRing(XEmacPs *InstancePtr)

detaileddescription.para: The corresponding interrupt for each bit set to 1 in Mask, will be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
contains a bit mask of interrupts to enable. The mask can be formed using a set of bitwise or'd values.

simplesect.para: The state of the transmitter and receiver are not modified by this function. C-style signature void XEmacPs_IntEnable(XEmacPs *InstancePtr, u32 Mask)

detaileddescription.para: The corresponding interrupt for each bit set to 1 in Mask, will be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
contains a bit mask of interrupts to disable. The mask can be formed using a set of bitwise or'd values.

simplesect.para: The state of the transmitter and receiver are not modified by this function. C-style signature void XEmacPs_IntDisable(XEmacPs *InstancePtr, u32 Mask)

briefdescription.para: Enable interrupts of queue Queue specified in Mask.

detaileddescription.para: The corresponding interrupt for each bit set to 1 in Mask, will be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
of Queue, it should be from 1.
contains a bit mask of interrupts to enable. The mask can be formed using a set of bitwise or'd values.

simplesect.para: The state of the transmitter and receiver are not modified by this function. C-style signature void XEmacPs_IntQiEnable(XEmacPs *InstancePtr, u8 Queue, u32 Mask)

briefdescription.para: Disable interrupts of queue Queue specified in Mask.

detaileddescription.para: The corresponding interrupt for each bit set to 1 in Mask, will be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
of Queue, it should be from 1.
contains a bit mask of interrupts to disable. The mask can be formed using a set of bitwise or'd values.

simplesect.para: The state of the transmitter and receiver are not modified by this function. C-style signature void XEmacPs_IntDisable(XEmacPs *InstancePtr, u8 Queue, u32 Mask)

briefdescription.para: This macro triggers transmit circuit to send data currently in TX buffer(s).

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XEmacPs instance to be worked on.
Signature: void XEmacPs_Transmit(XEmacPs *InstancePtr)

briefdescription.para: This macro determines if the device is configured with checksum offloading on the receive channel.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XEmacPs instance to be worked on.
Boolean TRUE if the device is configured with checksum offloading, or FALSE otherwise.
Signature: u32 XEmacPs_IsRxCsum(XEmacPs *InstancePtr)

briefdescription.para: This macro determines if the device is configured with checksum offloading on the transmit channel.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XEmacPs instance to be worked on.
Boolean TRUE if the device is configured with checksum offloading, or FALSE otherwise.
Signature: u32 XEmacPs_IsTxCsum(XEmacPs *InstancePtr)

briefdescription.para: This macro sets RX watermark register.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XEmacPs instance to be worked on.
High
is the non-zero RX high watermark value. When SRAM fill level is above this, a pause frame will be sent.
Low
is the non-zero RX low watermark value. When SRAM fill level is below this, a zero length pause frame will be sent IF the last pause frame sent was non-zero.
None
Signature: void XEmacPs_SetRXWatermark(XEmacPs *InstancePtr, u16 High, u16 Low)

briefdescription.para: This macro gets RX watermark register.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XEmacPs instance to be worked on.
RX watermark register value
Signature: void XEmacPs_GetRXWatermark(XEmacPs *InstancePtr)

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: Nothing
C-style signature: void XEmacPs_BdClear(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Read the given Buffer Descriptor word.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the BD to read
is the word offset to be read

simplesect.para: The 32-bit value of the field
C-style signature: u32 XEmacPs_BdRead(UINTPTR BaseAddress, UINTPTR Offset)

briefdescription.para: Write the given Buffer Descriptor word.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the BD to write
is the word offset to be written
is the 32-bit value to write to the field

simplesect.para: None.
C-style signature: void XEmacPs_BdWrite(UINTPTR BaseAddress, UINTPTR Offset, UINTPTR Data)

detaileddescription.para:
BdPtr
is the BD pointer to operate on
Addr
is the value to write to BD's status field.
: C-style signature: void XEmacPs_BdSetAddressTx(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr, UINTPTR Addr)

BdPtr
is the BD pointer to operate on
Addr
is the value to write to BD's status field.
: Due to some bits are mixed within receive BD's address field, read-modify-write is performed. C-style signature: void XEmacPs_BdSetAddressRx(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr, UINTPTR Addr)

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on
is the value to write to BD's status field.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XEmacPs_BdSetStatus(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr, UINTPTR Data)

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the BD's Packet DMA transfer status word (word 1).

detaileddescription.para:
BdPtr
is the BD pointer to operate on
Status word
C-style signature: u32 XEmacPs_BdGetStatus(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr) Due to the BD bit layout differences in transmit and receive. User's caution is required.

briefdescription.para: Get the address (bits 0..31) of the BD's buffer address (word 0)

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_BdGetBufAddr(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Set transfer length in bytes for the given BD.

detaileddescription.para: The length must be set each time a BD is submitted to hardware.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on
is the number of bytes to transfer.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XEmacPs_BdSetLength(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr, u32 LenBytes)

detaileddescription.para: For Tx channels, the returned value is the same as that written with XEmacPs_BdSetLength().
For Rx channels, the returned value is the size of the received packet.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: Length field processed by hardware or set by XEmacPs_BdSetLength().
C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_BdGetLength(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr) XEAMCPS_RXBUF_LEN_MASK is same as XEMACPS_TXBUF_LEN_MASK.

detaileddescription.para: The returned value is the size of the received packet. This API supports jumbo frame sizes if enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to XEmacps instance
is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: Length field processed by hardware or set by XEmacPs_BdSetLength().
C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_GetRxFrameSize(XEmacPs* InstancePtr, XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr) RxBufMask is dependent on whether jumbo is enabled or not.

briefdescription.para: Test whether the given BD has been marked as the last BD of a packet.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: TRUE if BD represents the "Last" BD of a packet, FALSE otherwise
C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_BdIsLast(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Tell the DMA engine that the given transmit BD marks the end of the current packet to be processed.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XEmacPs_BdSetLast(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Tell the DMA engine that the current packet does not end with the given BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XEmacPs_BdClearLast(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Set this bit to mark the last descriptor in the receive buffer descriptor list.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XEmacPs_BdSetRxWrap(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr) Determine the wrap bit of the receive BD which indicates end of the BD list.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u8 XEmacPs_BdIsRxWrap(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Sets this bit to mark the last descriptor in the transmit buffer descriptor list.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XEmacPs_BdSetTxWrap(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr) Determine the wrap bit of the transmit BD which indicates end of the BD list.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u8 XEmacPs_BdGetTxWrap(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Determine the new bit of the receive BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_BdIsRxNew(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Software sets this bit to disable the buffer to be read by the hardware.

detaileddescription.para: Hardware sets this bit for the first buffer of a frame once it has been successfully transmitted. This macro sets this bit of transmit BD to avoid confusion.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XEmacPs_BdSetTxUsed(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Software clears this bit to enable the buffer to be read by the hardware.

detaileddescription.para: Hardware sets this bit for the first buffer of a frame once it has been successfully transmitted. This macro clears this bit of transmit BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XEmacPs_BdClearTxUsed(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Determine the used bit of the transmit BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_BdIsTxUsed(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Determine if a frame fails to be transmitted due to too many retries.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_BdIsTxRetry(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Determine if a frame fails to be transmitted due to data can not be feteched in time or buffers are exhausted.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_BdIsTxUrun(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Determine if a frame fails to be transmitted due to buffer is exhausted mid-frame.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_BdIsTxExh(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Sets this bit, no CRC will be appended to the current frame.

detaileddescription.para: This control bit must be set for the first buffer in a frame and will be ignored for the subsequent buffers of a frame.

BdPtr
is the BD pointer to operate on
This bit must be clear when using the transmit checksum generation offload, otherwise checksum generation and substitution will not occur. C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_BdSetTxNoCRC(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Clear this bit, CRC will be appended to the current frame.

detaileddescription.para: This control bit must be set for the first buffer in a frame and will be ignored for the subsequent buffers of a frame.

BdPtr
is the BD pointer to operate on
This bit must be clear when using the transmit checksum generation offload, otherwise checksum generation and substitution will not occur. C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_BdClearTxNoCRC(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Determine the broadcast bit of the receive BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_BdIsRxBcast(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Determine the multicast hash bit of the receive BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_BdIsRxMultiHash(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Determine the unicast hash bit of the receive BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_BdIsRxUniHash(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Determine if the received frame is a VLAN Tagged frame.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_BdIsRxVlan(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Determine if the received frame has Type ID of 8100h and null VLAN identifier(Priority tag).

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_BdIsRxPri(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Determine if the received frame's Concatenation Format Indicator (CFI) of the frames VLANTCI field was set.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_BdIsRxCFI(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Determine the End Of Frame (EOF) bit of the receive BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_BdGetRxEOF(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Determine the Start Of Frame (SOF) bit of the receive BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD pointer to operate on

simplesect.para: C-style signature: UINTPTR XEmacPs_BdGetRxSOF(XEmacPs_Bd* BdPtr)

memberdef.name: XEMACPS_RING_SEEKAHEAD
memberdef.initializer: { \ UINTPTR Addr = (UINTPTR)(void *)(BdPtr); \ \ Addr += ((RingPtr)->Separation * (NumBd)); \ if ((Addr > (RingPtr)->HighBdAddr) || ((UINTPTR)(void *)(BdPtr) > Addr)) \ { \ Addr -= (RingPtr)->Length; \ } \ \ (BdPtr) = (XEmacPs_Bd*)(void *)Addr; \ }

memberdef.name: XEMACPS_RING_SEEKBACK
memberdef.initializer: { \ UINTPTR Addr = (UINTPTR)(void *)(BdPtr); \ \ Addr -= ((RingPtr)->Separation * (NumBd)); \ if ((Addr < (RingPtr)->BaseBdAddr) || ((UINTPTR)(void*)(BdPtr) < Addr)) \ { \ Addr += (RingPtr)->Length; \ } \ \ (BdPtr) = (XEmacPs_Bd*)(void*)Addr; \ }

briefdescription.para: Use this macro at initialization time to determine how many BDs will fit in a BD list within the given memory constraints.

detaileddescription.para: The results of this macro can be provided to XEmacPs_BdRingCreate().

parameterdescription.para: specifies what byte alignment the BDs must fall on and must be a power of 2 to get an accurate calculation (32, 64, 128,...)
is the number of bytes to be used to store BDs.

simplesect.para: Number of BDs that can fit in the given memory area
C-style signature: u32 XEmacPs_BdRingCntCalc(u32 Alignment, u32 Bytes)

briefdescription.para: Use this macro at initialization time to determine how many bytes of memory is required to contain a given number of BDs at a given alignment.

parameterdescription.para: specifies what byte alignment the BDs must fall on. This parameter must be a power of 2 to get an accurate calculation (32, 64, 128,...)
is the number of BDs to calculate memory size requirements for

simplesect.para: The number of bytes of memory required to create a BD list with the given memory constraints.
C-style signature: u32 XEmacPs_BdRingMemCalc(u32 Alignment, u32 NumBd)

briefdescription.para: Return the total number of BDs allocated by this channel with XEmacPs_BdRingCreate().

parameterdescription.para: is the DMA channel to operate on.

simplesect.para: The total number of BDs allocated for this channel.
C-style signature: u32 XEmacPs_BdRingGetCnt(XEmacPs_BdRing* RingPtr)

briefdescription.para: Return the number of BDs allocatable with XEmacPs_BdRingAlloc() for pre- processing.

parameterdescription.para: is the DMA channel to operate on.

simplesect.para: The number of BDs currently allocatable.
C-style signature: u32 XEmacPs_BdRingGetFreeCnt(XEmacPs_BdRing* RingPtr)

briefdescription.para: Return the next BD from BdPtr in a list.

parameterdescription.para: is the DMA channel to operate on.
is the BD to operate on.

simplesect.para: The next BD in the list relative to the BdPtr parameter.
C-style signature: XEmacPs_Bd XEmacPs_BdRingNext(XEmacPs_BdRing RingPtr, XEmacPs_Bd *BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Return the previous BD from BdPtr in the list.

parameterdescription.para: is the DMA channel to operate on.
is the BD to operate on

simplesect.para: The previous BD in the list relative to the BdPtr parameter.
C-style signature: XEmacPs_Bd XEmacPs_BdRingPrev(XEmacPs_BdRing RingPtr, XEmacPs_Bd *BdPtr)

briefdescription.para: Maxmum number of mac address supported.
Maxmum number of type id supported.
Minimum buffer descriptor alignment on the local bus.
Minimum buffer alignment when using options that impose alignment restrictions on the buffer data on the local bus.
Specify the receive buffer size in bytes, 64, 128, ...
Number of receive buffer bytes as a unit, this is HW setup.
Size of RX buffer descriptor queues.
Size of TX buffer descriptor queues.
Error-free larger than 1519 byte Frames transmitted counter.
Last statistic counter offset, for clearing.
RX Q1 DMA buffer size reg.
RX Q2 DMA buffer size reg.
RX Q3 DMA buffer size reg.
MSB Buffer TX Q Base reg.
MSB Buffer RX Q Base reg.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the register offset to be read

simplesect.para: The 32-bit value of the register
C-style signature: u32 XEmacPs_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset)

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the register offset to be written
is the 32-bit value to write to the register

simplesect.para: None.
C-style signature: void XEmacPs_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

detaileddescription.para: This file contains lookup method by device ID when success, it returns pointer to config table to be used to initialize the device.
This file contains the declarations of PCS block api's.
This file contains the implementation of the ethernet PCS block api's.
Functions in this file implement general purpose interrupt processing related functionality.
This header file contains identifiers and low-level driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the PS Ethernet MAC (XEmacPs) device.
This file contains the implementation of the ethernet interface reset sequence.
This file contains a configuration table that specifies the configuration of ethernet devices in the system.
Functions in this file implement general purpose command and control related functionality.
The Xiline EmacPs Buffer Descriptor ring driver.
This file implements buffer descriptor ring related functions.
This header provides operations to manage buffer descriptors in support of scatter-gather DMA.
Functions in this file are the minimum required functions for this driver. See xemacps.h for a detailed description of the driver.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a wsy 01/10/10 First release 2.1 srt 07/15/14 Add support for Zynq Ultrascale Mp GEM specification and 64-bit changes. 3.00 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance. 3.0 hk 02/20/15 Added support for jumbo frames. Increase AHB burst. Disable extended mode. Perform all 64 bit changes under check for arch64. 3.1 hk 08/10/15 Update upper 32 bit tx and rx queue ptr registers 3.5 hk 08/14/17 Update cache coherency information of the interface in its config structure. 3.8 hk 09/17/18 Cleanup stale comments. 3.8 mus 11/05/18 Support 64 bit DMA addresses for Microblaze-X platform. 3.10 hk 05/16/19 Clear status registers properly in reset 3.11 sd 02/14/20 Add clock support

detaileddescription.para: The API exported by this header defines abstracted macros that allow the user to read/write specific BD fields.

A buffer descriptor (BD) defines a DMA transaction. The macros defined by this header file allow access to most fields within a BD to tailor a DMA transaction according to user and hardware requirements. See the hardware IP DMA spec for more information on BD fields and how they affect transfers.
The XEmacPs_Bd structure defines a BD. The organization of this structure is driven mainly by the hardware for use in scatter-gather DMA transfers.
Limiting I/O to BDs can improve overall performance of the DMA channel.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes ----- ---- -------- ------------------------------------------------------- 1.00a wsy 01/10/10 First release 2.1 srt 07/15/14 Add support for Zynq Ultrascale MP GEM specification and 64-bit changes. 3.0 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance. 3.0 hk 02/20/15 Added support for jumbo frames. Disable extended mode. Perform all 64 bit changes under check for arch64. 3.2 hk 11/18/15 Change BD typedef and number of words. 3.8 hk 08/18/18 Remove duplicate definition of XEmacPs_BdSetLength 3.8 mus 11/05/18 Support 64 bit DMA addresses for Microblaze-X platform. 3.9 aj 22/03/24 Add mask for XEmaPs_BdGetBufAddr
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a wsy 01/10/10 First release 1.00a asa 11/21/11 The function XEmacPs_BdRingFromHwTx is modified. Earlier it used to search in "BdLimit" number of BDs to know which BDs are processed. Now one more check is added. It looks for BDs till the current BD pointer reaches HwTail. By doing this processing time is saved. 1.00a asa 01/24/12 The function XEmacPs_BdRingFromHwTx in file xemacps_bdring.c is modified. Now start of packet is searched for returning the number of BDs processed. 1.05a asa 09/23/13 Cache operations on BDs are not required and hence removed. It is expected that all BDs are allocated in from uncached area. Fix for CR #663885. 2.1 srt 07/15/14 Add support for Zynq Ultrascale Mp architecture. 3.0 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance. 3.6 rb 09/08/17 Add XEmacPs_BdRingPtrReset() API to reset BD ring pointers

detaileddescription.para: This is part of EmacPs DMA functionalities.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a wsy 01/10/10 First release 2.1 srt 07/15/14 Add support for Zynq Ultrascale Mp architecture. 3.0 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance. 3.6 rb 09/08/17 HwCnt variable (in XEmacPs_BdRing structure) is changed to volatile.

detaileddescription.para: See xemacps.h for a detailed description of the driver.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a wsy 01/10/10 First release 1.02a asa 11/05/12 Added a new API for deleting an entry from the HASH register. Added a new API for setting the BURST length in DMACR register. 2.1 srt 07/15/14 Add support for Zynq Ultrascale Mp architecture. 3.0 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance. 3.0 hk 02/20/15 Added support for jumbo frames. 3.2 hk 02/22/16 Added SGMII support for Zynq Ultrascale+ MPSoC.
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a wsy 01/10/10 First release 2.00 hk 22/01/14 Added check for picking second instance 3.00 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance.
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.05a kpc 28/06/13 First release 3.00 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance.

detaileddescription.para: High-level driver functions are defined in xemacps.h.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a wsy 01/10/10 First release. 1.02a asa 11/05/12 Added hash defines for DMACR burst length configuration. 1.05a kpc 28/06/13 Added XEmacPs_ResetHw function prototype 1.06a asa 11/02/13 Changed the value for XEMACPS_RXBUF_LEN_MASK from 0x3fff to 0x1fff. This fixes the CR#744902. 2.1 srt 07/15/14 Add support for Zynq Ultrascale Mp GEM specification. 3.0 kvn 12/16/14 Changed name of XEMACPS_NWCFG_LENGTHERRDSCRD_MASK to XEMACPS_NWCFG_LENERRDSCRD_MASK as it exceeds 31 characters. 3.0 kpc 1/23/15 Corrected the extended descriptor macro values. 3.0 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance. 3.0 hk 03/18/15 Added support for jumbo frames. Remove "used bit set" from TX error interrupt masks. 3.1 hk 08/10/15 Update upper 32 bit tx and rx queue ptr register offsets. 3.2 hk 02/22/16 Added SGMII support for Zynq Ultrascale+ MPSoC. 3.8 hk 09/17/18 Fix PTP interrupt masks. 3.9 hk 01/23/19 Add RX watermark support 3.10 hk 05/16/19 Clear status registers properly in reset 3.18 sne 01/11/23 Add PCS control and status registers information.

detaileddescription.para: See xemacps.h for a detailed description of the driver.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a wsy 01/10/10 First release 1.03a asa 01/24/13 Fix for CR #692702 which updates error handling for Rx errors. Under heavy Rx traffic, there will be a large number of errors related to receive buffer not available. Because of a HW bug (SI #692601), under such heavy errors, the Rx data path can become unresponsive. To reduce the probabilities for hitting this HW bug, the SW writes to bit 18 to flush a packet from Rx DPRAM immediately. The changes for it are done in the function XEmacPs_IntrHandler. 2.1 srt 07/15/14 Add support for Zynq Ultrascale Mp GEM specification and 64-bit changes. 3.0 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance. 3.1 hk 07/27/15 Do not call error handler with '0' error code when there is no error. CR# 869403
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
3.22 vineeth 11/15/24 First Release
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a wsy 01/10/10 New 3.00 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance.
